text:p.text:span: When Abaindasa, captain of the armed forces,
the punishment that you imposed on him.
Why did you remove him from that regiment
Having reduced the punishment that you had imposed by your own volition,
now that the enemy is coming against you, reinstate him into that regiment!
] Ur-Namma, the father who begot me,
As soon as my letter concerning him has been sent,
You must not diminish (the importance) of that city:
its importance to that regiment is something that has (already) has been explained to you,
so you should install him in a stronghold before you as if on a throne!
X1 has deteriorated since it was copied and since I first collated it in 1985.

text:note-body.text:p: Much of the decay has affected the upper part of the reverse. The current state of the tablet can be seen on the digital photographs included here that were kindly supplied by E. Robson; a scan of an older museum photograph is included for comparison. Then transliteration is based on the earlier state.

office:text.text:p: Tablet typology: Prism: N1, Type III: X1.

text:p.text:span: N1 o o o o o o o o o o o . . o o

office:text.text:p: X1 o o o . . o o + + +

text:p.text:span: X1 o o o o o . + + o o .
Followed by the traces of a line on lower edge.

office:text.text:p: X1 o o o o o . + + +
N1 o x + + + + - . o o + + o o

text:note-body.text:p: There had to be half a line of text before this in X1.
As in the line above, in X1 this version had words at the beginning of this line that are not present in N1.

office:text.text:p: N1 Omits or on previous line.
X1 o o o o o o o . . +
N1 + + ki + + + . o + + + + h[é]o o o o o
N1 omits or on previous line.

text:p.text:span: The reconstruction of this letter is highly tentative, and there may be less harmony between the two sources than meets the eye. X1 has more of the text than N1 but is an inferior manuscript with many errors. The difficulties are compounded by the current state of preservation of the former, which has deteriorated markedly since O. R. Gurney made his copy, especially on the reverse. The translation offered here is highly provisional, and everything is subject to revision. The pronominal elements in verbal forms are difficult, and third person may be used when first person is intended; therefore
may have to be rendered as “his regiment,” rather than as “that regiment.” It is possible that this and
Ar2 (letter 5) are variant recensions of the same letter.
8 (1990) 109–11, is used regularly in­ administrative letters but not in literary ones. The only other occurrence is in letter SEpM 4: 4, together with
. The unreliable source X1 has the form
in line 14 but this is hardly correct; N1 clearly had something else but only the sign
, has been ­vigorously discussed, most recently by M. Civil,
75–78, who­ argues for the meaning “capital offense.” The use of this noun with the verb
is otherwise unattested in Sumerian but it may be a calque of Akkadian
, I solidify the foundations of my great far-flung lands
but this is hardly certain. The interpretation of
is problematical as there is no other evidence for /d
/ as the final consonant of the root.

before the finite verb form, preserved only in X1, makes little sense, as one would expect the verb to be subordinated. One cannot be certain but the poor remains of the equivalent sign in N1 resemble
. The interpretation of this line, as well as the preceding ones, is highly conjectural, and admittedly makes little sense. The two sources are not in perfect harmony. For
, possibly “secure place, stronghold,” see S. Tinney,
, and therefore perhaps we should think of Izi C iii 12–13 [
